page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. This CPU provides 32 physical cores and 64 virtual processors (vCPUs) with a base frequency of 4.2 GHz. The memory configuration consists of 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_tile and AMX_int8).

This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be fulfilled via external network-attached storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring low-latency local scratch space, necessitating reliance on remote storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the D64s_v7 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, scoring 73215.97 in stress-ng multi-core tests and 89862.33 in PassMark CPU tests, placing it in the top 10% tier. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2269.0. Memory bandwidth is robust, reaching 332.29 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Database and key-value store performance is notable, with Redis SET operations achieving 116.73 million operations per second and PassMark database operations scoring in the top 10% tier. Static web serving throughput is also high at 86.42 GB/sec.

Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio and strong multi-threaded capabilities, the D64s_v7 is appropriate for enterprise-grade database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. It also supports moderate machine learning inference tasks, processing 219.65 tokens per second for medium-sized 7B models during prompt processing, though text generation speeds are more modest.
description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it delivers 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. The CPU features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions. While it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. In benchmarks, the instance shows strong multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 10% for PassMark CPU and database operations, and achieving 116.73 million operations per second in Redis SET tests. This profile makes it appropriate for enterprise database hosting, high-throughput static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor, delivering top-tier database and multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for databases and web serving.
tagline: A balanced 64-vCPU virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and robust memory bandwidth for enterprise databases and web serving.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier database performance with 116.73 million Redis SET operations per second
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Appropriate for enterprise databases, high-throughput web serving, and multi-threaded processing
